# Suggested GitHub Labels

This is a lightweight label set for `FL Plates`. The goal is enough structure to stay organized without turning the repo into bureaucracy.

## Type Labels

- `bug` — `#d73a4a`
  Description: Something is broken or behaving incorrectly.
- `enhancement` — `#a2eeef`
  Description: New feature or meaningful improvement.
- `design` — `#c5def5`
  Description: Visual, layout, or interaction design work.
- `data` — `#bfdadc`
  Description: Plate catalog, metadata, category, or image update.
- `docs` — `#f9d0c4`
  Description: Documentation, notes, roadmap, or release process update.

## Area Labels

- `ui` — `#1d76db`
  Description: Main interface, panels, modals, controls, or layout.
- `badges` — `#7057ff`
  Description: Merit badge rules, badge UI, or badge content.
- `plates` — `#0e8a16`
  Description: Plate tiles, plate versions, plate images, or plate details.
- `search` — `#5319e7`
  Description: Search behavior, search terms, and discovery lookup.
- `map` — `#006b75`
  Description: Map, pins, regions, geography, or spatial display.
- `settings` — `#fbca04`
  Description: Settings, preferences, toggles, and utility controls.
- `release` — `#b60205`
  Description: Versioning, release prep, deployment, and shipping tasks.

## Priority Labels

- `priority:high` — `#b60205`
  Description: Important and should be handled soon.
- `priority:medium` — `#fbca04`
  Description: Worth doing soon, but not urgent.
- `priority:low` — `#0e8a16`
  Description: Nice to have or can wait.

## Status Labels

- `needs-review` — `#d4c5f9`
  Description: Needs product, UX, or data review before implementation.
- `blocked` — `#000000`
  Description: Cannot move forward until something else is resolved.
- `good first task` — `#7057ff`
  Description: Small, well-bounded task and a good easy starting point.

## Practical Usage

Usually:

- pick `1` type label
- pick `1` area label

Examples:

- category cleanup: `data` + `plates`
- badge logic change: `enhancement` + `badges`
- modal spacing issue: `bug` + `ui`
- search keyword work: `enhancement` + `search`
- release prep: `docs` + `release`
